= Drink Recipes =

== Contents ==
- [[#Drink Recipes]]
  - [[#Margarita]]
  - [[#Martini]]
    - [[#Dry Martini]]
    - [[#Perfect Martini]]
    - [[#White Lady]]
  - [[#Tea]]
    - [[#Karak Chai]]
  - [[#Backlinks]]

== Margarita ==
:margarita:

Adapted from
[[https://www.simplyrecipes.com/recipes/classic_margarita/|Classic Margarita]].
The proportions doubled to fit my cocktail glass. The lime juice doubled again
to balance the añejo tequila.

- 3 oz tequila
- 2 oz Cointreau
- 2 oz lime juice

== Martini ==
:martini:

=== Dry Martini ===

- 6 part gin
- 1 part dry vermouth
- 2 dash orange bitters
- 1 inch strip of peel from an un-waxed lemon or skewered olives

=== Perfect Martini ===

- [[https://www.diffordsguide.com/cocktails/recipe/1509/perfect-martini|Diffordsguide Cocktails Recipe Perfect Martini]]

- 4 part gin
- 1/2 part dry vermouth
- 1/2 part sweet vermouth
- 1 dash orange bitters
- 1 inch strip of peel from an un-waxed lemon or skewered olives

=== White Lady ===

- 4 parts Empress 1898 Indigo gin
- 1 part lime or lemon juice
- 1.5 part Cointreau
- Pomegranate arils or orange peel

Shake for 10 seconds with ice and garnish with pomegranate arils or orange
peel.

== Tea ==
:tea:

CTC:: crush, tear, curl is black tea processing method.

=== Karak Chai ===
:chai:milk:masala:

Derived from [[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nFWgmhIxSKY|The Secret INGREDIENT Chaiwala Karak Chai]].
Scaled for 2900 ml (No. 10 can) coconut milk, substituted for milk.

*Ingredients*:

- Milk – 2900 ml
- Water – 2200 ml
- Cardamom – 15 pods
- Cloves – 15 pieces
- Ginger – 4 small slice (1 Tb [[https://www.penzeys.com/online-catalog/penzeys-minced-ginger/c-24/p-1024/pd-s|Penzeys Minced Ginger]])
- Cinnamon – 4 small stick
- [[cooking#Mignonette Pepper|Mignonette Pepper]] – 1 Tb
- CTC black tea – 1 cup
- Sugar – 1 cup

*Instructions*:

1. Crack cardamom pods.
2. Lightly crush remaining spices.
3. Add water and spices to 10 qt stock pot.
4. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
5. Lower the heat and add black tea, cardamom, and cloves.
6. Let it simmer gently for 5 minutes. Avoid boiling to prevent bitterness.
7. Add milk and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes.
8. Add sugar to taste.
9. Strain into serving teapot.

== Backlinks ==
- [[/index|John D. Fisher's Home Wiki]]
- [[/Topic_Index|Topic_Index]]
- [[/cooking|Cooking]]
